Scenario.title: Water Treatment System Hacked via Phishing and SCADA Exploit

overview.description: The attack begins with a phishing campaign targeting employees, followed by exploitation of SCADA system vulnerabilities to disrupt operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Adversaries send phishing emails to employees, leveraging CVE-2025-4632 to gain initial access. The spear phishing attack targets specific users to maximize deception and exploit known vulnerabilities in email handling processes.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 523 Name: Malicious Software Implanted
stage.description: Once inside, attackers exploit CVE-2023-41993 to gain control over SCADA systems. This allows them to execute arbitrary commands and manipulate system configurations, leading to potential operational disruptions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Attackers modify data within SCADA databases, potentially altering chemical concentrations in water treatment processes. This tampering can lead to unsafe water quality conditions or system malfunctions.

Scenario.summary: The attack starts with a phishing email exploiting CVE-2025-4632, followed by SCADA system exploitation using CVE-2023-41993, ultimately resulting in data tampering that disrupts water treatment operations.
